# Bitcoin Institute

> The Satoshi Nakamoto reference — primary sources, conversations, and editorial readings.

Bitcoin Institute is a bilingual (English/Japanese) reference site that collects and preserves primary-source material on Satoshi Nakamoto, the pseudonymous creator of Bitcoin, alongside editorial readings of source code, primary records, and Satoshi's documented footprint. Coverage spans the cypherpunks-era predecessors through the post-Satoshi years.

## Contents

The archive contains 3,100+ entries organized by type:

- **Forum Posts** (2,609): BitcoinTalk (2,531), GitHub PRs (64), SourceForge (9), P2P Foundation (4), including Satoshi's posts and context posts from other participants
- **Correspondence** (337): Private emails between Satoshi and 9 key participants — Adam Back, Dustin Trammell, Gavin Andresen, Hal Finney, Laszlo Hanyecz, Martti Malmi, Mike Hearn, Nicholas Bohm, Wei Dai
- **Articles** (73): Media coverage, research, and post-disappearance historical analysis
- **Mailing List** (46): Cryptography mailing list (28), Bitcoin-list (16), P2P Research (4)
- **Biographies** (32): Biographical profiles of key participants compiled from public sources
- **BIPs** (11): Bitcoin Improvement Proposals relevant to Satoshi's design
- **Analysis** (8): Editorial readings by Bitcoin Institute on Satoshi's intellectual position relative to the cypherpunk movement, the techno-orientalist symbolic field of the "Satoshi Nakamoto" pseudonym, the genesis block hardcode mechanism, the v0.1 source code, the launch-period operational environment, distribution and tooling anomalies, and Satoshi's self-statements 2008-2011
- **Court Documents** (3): COPA v Wright trial materials
- **Whitepapers** (2): Bitcoin whitepaper draft and final version

## Key Pages

- Home: https://bitcoin-institute.pages.dev/
- All Entries: https://bitcoin-institute.pages.dev/entries/
- Analyses: https://bitcoin-institute.pages.dev/types/analysis/
- Sources: https://bitcoin-institute.pages.dev/sources/
- Participants: https://bitcoin-institute.pages.dev/participants/
- Tags: https://bitcoin-institute.pages.dev/tags/
- Search: https://bitcoin-institute.pages.dev/search/
- About: https://bitcoin-institute.pages.dev/about/
- Price Chart: https://bitcoin-institute.pages.dev/chart/

## Japanese Version

- Home: https://bitcoin-institute.pages.dev/ja/
- All Entries: https://bitcoin-institute.pages.dev/ja/entries/
- Analyses: https://bitcoin-institute.pages.dev/ja/types/analysis/
- Search: https://bitcoin-institute.pages.dev/ja/search/
- Price Chart: https://bitcoin-institute.pages.dev/ja/chart/

## Key Facts

- Satoshi Nakamoto was active from October 2008 to April 2011
- Satoshi mined an estimated 1.1 million BTC, none of which have ever been moved
- The last known email was to Gavin Andresen on April 26, 2011, transferring the network alert key: "I've moved on to other things and will probably be unavailable." Three days earlier (April 23), Satoshi told Mike Hearn "I've moved on to other things. It's in good hands with Gavin and everyone."
- All primary entries include original source URLs for verification; editorial analyses cite their sources for each claim
